behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing — missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is orchestration: validation must both aggregate missing fields and stop downstream modules from running. A unit test of only the validator could miss accidental downstream calls.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the intake orchestrator with test doubles for insurance, triage, scheduling, and confirmation; submit input missing multiple required fields; assert returned error lists all missing fields and assert no downstream module was invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is local parsing/range logic in one validation component with no module-boundary dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Test the DOB validator function directly with table-driven cases: future date, >150 years old, invalid string, and valid boundary dates.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets the "eligible" vs "not eligible" vs "pending" response.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real HTTP request/response contract behavior; pure mocks can hide serialization/path/header mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a local HTTP test server that records request payload and returns each status variant; assert outbound patient ID/plan code are correct and mapped statuses are interpreted correctly.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as "pending" and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Unreachable-network behavior is an HTTP/transport failure mode; mocking exceptions can miss real timeout/connection handling paths.
behaviors.test_description: Point insurance client to a non-listening endpoint (or force connection timeout) in an end-to-end intake run; assert eligibility becomes pending and intake proceeds to later steps.

behaviors.description: The AI symptom triage correctly assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: The behavior is about model judgment quality, which depends on real AI output and can vary across runs.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real triage model with emergency symptom sets and assert urgency is classified high using acceptance thresholds over repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The symptom triage produces a natural language explanation of its urgency assessment that a clinician can review, not just a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Explanation quality and presence as clinician-readable language depend on real model generation, not only schema wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real triage engine and assert output includes a non-trivial natural-language rationale tied to symptoms (not only numeric/label fields), evaluated across multiple runs/prompts.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level — high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at scheduler + calendar DB interaction (slot search/order + urgency window rules), not single-function logic alone.
behaviors.test_description: Use a seeded local calendar DB with controlled availability; run scheduler with urgency inputs and assert chosen slot is earliest valid one and meets same-day/48h constraints.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are being processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: This is a race-condition boundary between concurrent scheduler calls and DB constraints/transactions; unit tests cannot expose real contention behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real local calendar DB, launch two concurrent scheduling requests for one open slot; assert only one booking succeeds and the other is re-routed/rejected without duplicate reservation.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes the patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to the visit type.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is message composition logic from structured inputs; no external I/O is required to catch missing fields or wrong instruction mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Test the confirmation builder with representative visit types and appointment metadata; assert required fields are present and prep instructions map correctly per visit type.

behaviors.description: The full intake pipeline — validate, check insurance, triage symptoms, schedule, confirm — completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an end-to-end multi-step pipeline that includes an agentic triage step plus external/API and DB interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the complete intake flow in a production-like test environment with real triage model and real integrations (payer endpoint substitute + shared calendar DB), then assert successful completion and emitted confirmation.
